name: George Handy
abstract: George Handy (born George Joseph Hendleman) (January 17, 1920 – January 8, 1997) was an American jazz arranger, composer and pianist whose musical beginnings were fostered under the tutelage of composer Aaron Copland. While he had an impressive career as a pianist, he is best known in retrospect for his bebop arrangements.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: On that band was ... George Handy was writing charts for the band... And then I went back out and joined Woody’s band again... before that I was with Boyd Raeburn’s band... George Handy was doing charts and people like that
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions George Handy; Interviewee responds 'Right. I did them for McKinley... They didn't even do them in Sauter-Finegan.' and later 'And for Pete Rugolo too.'

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, the way I heard it, that they kept you waiting forever while George Handy was on... [INTERVIEWEE] John Handy.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: He had a piano player that wrote real good. His name was John Handy -- George Handy. George was an arranger on that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Dave was there with an arranger named George Handy... George Handy was right in with us. He was in with the whole thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: he came in and with all those really wild charts and the two of us were writing for the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
